description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined, angular fuselage with a dark, camouflage-like exterior, suggesting a military or tactical design. The main rotor consists of two long, straight blades mounted atop a central hub, while the tail section extends back to a small, enclosed tail rotor assembly. The cockpit area is integrated into the front of the fuselage, with subtle window outlines that blend into the body, giving it a stealthy appearance. The landing gear appears to be a pair of robust skids, designed for stability on rough terrain. The surface is predominantly dark with hints of green and brown, and the overall build suggests a focus on durability and low visibility. No visible registration numbers or prominent markings are present on the exterior.
description: A dark, angular helicopter with camouflage coloring, two main rotor blades, a compact tail rotor, and sturdy landing skids. The cockpit windows are subtly integrated, and the design suggests a military or tactical use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, both main rotor blades, and the extended tail section with the tail rotor visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow, pointed nose, cockpit area, and the width of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, showing the other profile of the fuselage and tail assembly.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail rotor assembly, the back of the fuselage, and the alignment of the main rotor blades.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, straight main rotor blades made of composite or metal, dark in color with no visible markings, each blade extending nearly the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present at the end of the tail boom, enclosed in a protective structure, with multiple short blades arranged in a circular pattern, dark in color.
attribute_landing_skids: Pair of robust, straight landing skids made of metal, dark in color, running parallel beneath the fuselage with visible support struts.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Cockpit windows are present, angular and integrated into the front fuselage, appearing as dark, tinted panels with minimal visible framing.
